stemHtml: In the evening, the most common reason for the dissipation of land-based fair-weather cumulus clouds is:

options.a: The reduced convective activity of the air mass due to an increased environmental lapse rate.
options.b: Cooling of the surface which stops the convection and increases the stability of the air mass.
options.c: Formation of a tropopause inversion which creates a stable layer in the upper troposphere.
options.d: The divergence in the upper troposphere.
